title: Echinocoryne holosericea
intro: Echinocoryne holosericea is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as a type of thistle or prickly flower. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Echinocoryne holosericea include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Echinocoryne holosericea include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote digestion and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
